name: Vanuatu
avoid: November–March is wetter, more humid, and more cyclone-prone, so domestic flight reliability, boat crossings, and volcano visibility can all drop.
Outer-island schedules shift more than in Fiji or New Zealand, so same-day domestic connections need extra buffer and soft luggage helps on smaller aircraft.
Cash is still important outside Port Vila and Luganville, especially for blue-hole entries, village visits, local buses, and guesthouse meals.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, keep the plan to Efate plus either Tanna or Espiritu Santo; adding both outer-island hops makes the routing feel too flight-heavy.

cities.name: Port Vila
cities.notes: Port Vila on Efate is the easy arrival base for cascades, reef day trips, waterfront restaurants, local buses, and the short ferry to Hideaway Island.

cities.name: Tanna
cities.notes: Tanna is Vanuatu's volcanic showstopper for Mount Yasur, kastom village visits, Blue Cave excursions, and black-sand beaches around Port Resolution.

cities.name: Espiritu Santo
cities.notes: Espiritu Santo mixes the country's best blue holes, Champagne Beach, Millennium Cave adventures, and classic wreck snorkeling around Luganville.

cities.name: Ambrym
cities.notes: Ambrym is the rugged add-on for black-sand coasts, village culture, and rustic guesthouse stays closer to Vanuatu's more remote island rhythm.

itinerary.sim: Digicel Vanuatu or Vodafone Vanuatu prepaid both work around Port Vila and Luganville; buy a SIM on Efate, then download offline maps and airline confirmations before heading to Tanna or Ambrym.

Port Vila.name: Port Vila
Port Vila.note: Efate works best as a soft landing: one easy waterfall-and-snorkel day, then one cultural or lagoon day before you commit to outer-island flights.

days.theme: Mele Cascades, Underwater Postcards and a Waterfront Sunset

activities.name: Mele Cascades entry
activities.tip: Adult entry is commonly 2,000 VUV; carry swim shoes because the upper cascade track is rocky and slippery.

activities.name: Hideaway Island day pass for the Underwater Post Office
activities.tip: The day pass is usually around 1,200 VUV and includes the short ferry from Mele Bay; reef shoes help on the coral shoreline.

activities.name: Waterproof postcard mailed from the Underwater Post Office
activities.tip: A waterproof postcard usually costs around 660 VUV and is the easiest souvenir if visibility is good.

hotels.name: Ramada Resort by Wyndham Port Vila

days.theme: Port Vila Market, Local Buses and a Slower Lagoon Day

activities.name: Port Vila Market produce and handicraft stop
Local minibus rides around Port Vila and Mele
activities.tip: Town buses typically charge small per-seat cash fares, making them the cheapest way to move between central Port Vila and Mele.

activities.name: Erakor Island ferry and lagoon time
activities.tip: The short ferry crossing is inexpensive, while kayaks or SUP hire on the island pushes the spend higher.

activities.name: Ekasup Cultural Village visit
activities.tip: Most guided village visits land around 4,000–5,000 VUV depending on whether transport is bundled.

Tanna.name: Tanna
Tanna.note: Tanna deserves an overnight because Mount Yasur is best timed for late afternoon light, while kastom visits and the east-coast Blue Cave need their own weather window.

days.theme: Air Vanuatu Hop, Port Resolution and Mount Yasur at Sunset

activities.name: Air Vanuatu flight from Port Vila to Tanna
activities.tip: One-way fares often start around 17,600 VUV on the ATR route; remember the VT200 provincial departure tax is paid separately.

activities.name: Mount Yasur volcano access
activities.tip: The community entrance fee is typically 9,800 VUV, while 4WD transfers from White Grass or Lenakel are charged separately.

activities.name: Port Resolution hot springs and black-sand beach stop
activities.tip: This is usually bundled into a half-day driver or volcano transfer around the Port Resolution side of the island.

days.theme: Kastom Village, Blue Cave and a Slower Tanna Morning

activities.name: Yakel Custom Village visit
activities.tip: Guided village visits commonly land around 5,000–7,000 VUV depending on transport and whether a custom dance is included.

activities.name: Blue Cave boat excursion
activities.tip: Sea conditions decide departures on the east coast, so book with your resort and carry a dry bag.

Espiritu Santo.name: Espiritu Santo
Espiritu Santo.note: Santo is the most flexible outer-island stop: keep one blue-hole day, one cave or wreck day, and one beach day rather than rushing every north-coast sight in one loop.

days.theme: Flight via Port Vila, Blue Holes and an Aore Sunset

activities.name: Air Vanuatu routing from Tanna to Espiritu Santo via Port Vila
activities.tip: Most itineraries use separate Tanna–Port Vila and Port Vila–Santo sectors, so keep buffer time in case the first leg shifts.

activities.name: Nanda Blue Hole entry
activities.tip: Community entry is commonly around 1,500 VUV; cash is preferred and the water is clearest before midday.

activities.name: Matevulu Blue Hole entry
activities.tip: Expect about 1,000–1,500 VUV depending on the access point and whether you hire a local tube or guide.

activities.name: Aore Island ferry across the Segond Channel
activities.tip: The crossing is short and usually paid in cash at the dock or through your accommodation.

days.theme: Millennium Cave Trek and Luganville Waterfront Recovery

activities.name: Millennium Cave guided trek
activities.tip: Most guided day treks land around 3,500–5,000 VUV and require proper shoes because the route mixes river sections, ladders, and limestone.

activities.name: Million Dollar Point snorkel entry
activities.tip: Community access fees are usually around 1,000–2,000 VUV; visibility is best when the sea is calm and bright.

days.theme: Champagne Beach, Riri Blue Hole and a Final Santo Swim Day

activities.name: Champagne Beach community entry
activities.tip: Adult entry is usually around 500–1,000 VUV, and the road is easiest to combine with another east-coast stop.

activities.name: Riri Blue Hole swim stop
activities.tip: Expect roughly 1,000–1,500 VUV for community access; bring a rope swing or tube mindset rather than a rushed photo stop.

activities.name: Aore or east-coast reef snorkel
activities.tip: Boat-based snorkel trips vary widely by resort, but short guided reef outings generally stay within this band.

Ambrym.name: Ambrym
Ambrym.note: Ambrym works best as a rustic extension once you've already seen Efate and Santo: come for guesthouse life, village culture, and a slower pace rather than polished resort infrastructure.

days.theme: Craig Cove Arrival, Village Culture and the Black-Sand Coast

activities.name: Local passenger boat from Santo or limited outer-island air connection into Ambrym
activities.tip: Transport is weather- and schedule-dependent, so most guesthouses reconfirm the latest boat day or small-aircraft seat before arrival.

activities.name: Fanla village cultural visit
activities.tip: Visits are usually arranged through your host and often include a small community payment rather than a formal ticket office.

days.theme: Optional Extra Day for West-Coast Boats and Volcano-Logistics Planning

activities.name: Local boat outing between coastal villages
activities.tip: Short inter-village boat rides are usually arranged on the spot and priced by boat rather than by published timetable.

activities.name: 4WD transfer toward North Ambrym trailheads
activities.tip: Only worth paying for if you're arranging a longer volcano trek with a guide; otherwise keep Ambrym as a culture-and-coast stop.

connections.method: Local bus or short taxi ride into central Port Vila, Mele, or lagoon-side resorts on Efate.
Air Vanuatu domestic flight from Bauerfield to Whitegrass, followed by a resort transfer or local 4WD pickup.
Air Vanuatu usually routes this via Port Vila, often as separate domestic sectors, so build in buffer time for the hub transfer.
Use a local passenger boat when the sea is settled, or take a limited outer-island Air Vanuatu service with light-aircraft baggage limits.
Return by outer-island Air Vanuatu service, sometimes via Santo, or by boat if your schedule is flexible and seas cooperate.

extras: Carry more VUV cash than you would in Fiji because community-run attractions, local buses, guesthouse meals, and some small-island transfers often do not take cards.
Air Vanuatu's smaller outer-island services can shift with weather and aircraft availability, so avoid tight same-day international connections after Ambrym or Tanna.
Dry bags, reef shoes, and a lightweight headlamp are genuinely useful here because blue holes, cave treks, and boat landings are more rustic than resort-heavy Pacific destinations.
April to October is the best overall weather window, but evenings on Tanna and Santo can still feel cool after rain or on open boats, so pack one light layer.
